# Provider Session Preservation

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/providerruntimeboundarydocs -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to runtime actor boundary](../runtime-actor-boundary.md)

C4 Provider Runtime owns the provider session table that maps provider-native session identity to Riido runtime identity. C5 Scheduling owns task claim lease and heartbeat semantics, not this table.

| Pair key | Preservation location |
| --- | --- |
| (`TaskID`, `RunID`, `ProviderSessionID`) | IR `SessionPinned` event payload plus C5 lease metadata runtime pin |
| (`ProviderSessionID`, `RuntimeID`) | C4 provider session table `riido-provider-session-table.v1` |

- Adapters do not keep session id as hidden private memory; they emit it as a draft for ingest.
- Crash resume is recovered from the IR log plus provider session table.
- The minimal session table key is `(ProviderSessionID, RuntimeID)`.
- Runtime eligibility, lease expiry, stale fingerprint, and task handoff are still owned by C5/C9.
